titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 579

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=900 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.58709+00 | translation moa=901 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.605741+00

p.cl: M . Rex Scott’ . Priori de Goldingham salutem .

Sciatis quod uolo et firmiter precipio ut
plenarie habeatis omnem libertatem quam uobis priori carta mea confirmaui scilicet . ut habe
atis bosca uestra et Garenniam uestram per terram uestram et ut de Boscis uestris aut de Ga
rennia uestra nullus intromittere . uel in illis quicumque capere presumat nisi per uos . et siquis forte hanc
uestram libertatem infringere presumpserit  super meam plenariam forisfacturam hoc faciet .
Malcolm, king of the Scots, to the prior of Coldingham, greeting.

Know that I will and resolutely instruct that you fully have every liberty that I have confirmed to you by my charter, that is to say, that you have your wood and warren throughout your land and that in respect of your woods and your warren no one presume to intrude or to enter them unless by your permission, and if by chance anyone may have undertaken to violate this liberty, he shall do it on pain of my full forfeiture.
As witnesses: Walter the chancellor, David Oliphant.
